Plaintiff'S Notice Of Motion And Motion For Preliminary Injunction
Off Calendar May-14-2021 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
This matter is called with the following cases: CGC-20-586732 (People vs. Aguilar-Benegas); CGC-20-586761 (People vs. Zelaya); and CGC-20-586763 (People vs. Padilla-Martel).

Appearances (via ZOOM videoconference):
Atty. Peter Keith, Esq. of the Office of the City Attorney, 415.554.3908, for the People of the State of California;
Atty. Mandy Chan, Esq. of DLA Piper LLC, 415.836.2500, for the Defendant(s);
Atty. Annie Decker, Esq. of the American Civil Liberties Union Foundation of Northern California, 415.621.2493, for the Defendant(s).

PLAINTIFF PEOPLE OF THE STATE OF CALIFORNIA'S Motion For Preliminary Injunction.

The matters are called. The Court informs counsel that prior to the hearing this afternoon it emailed to counsel its Order Regarding Hearing On Plaintiff's Motions For Preliminary Injunction filed this date. Portions of the order are then read into the record by the Court.
ARGUMENT is held. At the conclusion of argument, the matters are taken under submission.

The Court inquires about the status of the twenty-eight lawsuits that were filed noting that these are the first four of those lawsuits. Mr. Keith provides a brief update for the Court on the lawsuits noting that these are the first four cases that aren't in Federal Court. The Court addresses the filing of the amicus brief over objections and allows counsel the opportunity to reply. Mr. Keith states that they will not respond to the amicus brief. Brief discussions regarding the status of the lawsuits are held.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman; Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda; Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR No. 8600, Email: maria.torreano@gmail.com. (302/EPS)
